initial.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

Let me start with the ASP encoding:

```asp
% Find the 4s
four(R, C) :- input(R, C, 4).

% Find the rectangle defined by the 4s
min_row_rect(R) :- four(R, _), R = #min{R2 : four(R2, _)}.
max_row_rect(R) :- four(R, _), R = #max{R2 : four(R2, _)}.
min_col_rect(C) :- four(_, C), C = #min{C2 : four(_, C2)}.
max_col_rect(C) :- four(_, C), C = #max{C2 : four(_, C2)}.

% Crop to the rectangle
cropped(R, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), min_row_rect(Rmin), max_row_rect(Rmax), min_col_rect(Cmin), max_col_rect(Cmax), R >= Rmin, R <= Rmax, C >= Cmin, C <= Cmax.

% Output dimensions
out_rows(N) :- N = #max{R : cropped(R, _, _)}.
out_cols(M) :- M = #max{C : cropped(_, C, _)}.

% Output has the same dimensions as the cropped rectangle
output_cell(R, C) :- cropped(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
```

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

```asp
% Find the bounding box defined by 4s
min_4_row(R) :- input(R, _, 4), not input(R2, _, 4), R2 < R.
max_4_row(R) :- input(R, _, 4), not input(R2, _, 4), R2 > R.
min_4_col(C) :- input(_, C, 4), not input(_, C2, 4), C2 < C.
max_4_col(C) :- input(_, C, 4), not input(_, C2, 4), C2 > C.

% Output size is the bounding box size
n_rows(N) :- N = max_4_row(R) - min_4_row(R) + 1.
n_cols(C) :- C = max_4_col(C) - min_4_col(C) + 1.

% Find all shapes inside the bounding box
% A shape is a connected component of non-zero, non-4 cells inside the bounding box
% For simplicity, I'll assume all shapes are rectangles (which seems to be the case in the examples)

% Find all non-zero, non-4 cells inside the bounding box
inside_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, V != 4,
    min_4_row(MR), max_4_row(MR2), min_4_col(MC), max_4_col(MC2),
    R >= MR, R <= MR2, C >= MC, C <= MC2.

% Find all non-zero, non-4 cells outside the bounding box
outside_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, V != 4,
    min_4_row(MR), max_4_row(MR2), min_4_col(MC), max_4_col(MC2),
    not (R >= MR, R <= MR2, C >= MC, C <= MC2).

% Extract the outside shape
% The outside shape is the bounding box of all outside cells
min_out_row(R) :- R = #min{R : outside_cell(R, _)}.
max_out_row(R) :- R = #max{R : outside_cell(R, _)}.
min_out_col(C) :- C = #min{C : outside_cell(_, C)}.
max_out_col(C) :- C = #max{C : outside_cell(_, C)}.

% The outside pattern is the grid of cells in the outside shape
outside_pattern(R, C, V) :-
    min_out_row(MR), max_out_row(MR2), min_out_col(MC), max_out_col(MC2),
    R = MR..MR2, C = MC..MC2,
    input(R, C, V).

% Determine the grid size from the outside pattern
grid_rows(N) :- N = max_out_row(R) - min_out_row(R) + 1.
grid_cols(N) :- N = max_out_col(C) - min_out_col(C) + 1.

% Determine the shape size from the inside shapes
% For simplicity, I'll assume all shapes have the same size
shape_rows(N) :- N = #max{R : inside_cell(R, _)} - #min{R : inside_cell(R, _)} + 1.
shape_cols(N) :- N = #max{C : inside_cell(_, C)} - #min{C : inside_cell(_, C)} + 1.

% Place shapes in the output according to the outside pattern
```

The key rules are:
1. The 4s define a bounding box.

3. Extract shapes from inside and outside the bounding box.

```asp
% Find the bounding box defined by 4s
min_4_row(R) :- input(R, _, 4), not input(R2, _, 4), R2 < R.
max_4_row(R) :- input(R, _, 4), not input(R2, _, 4), R2 > R.
min_4_col(C) :- input(_, C, 4), not input(_, C2, 4), C2 < C.
max_4_col(C) :- input(_, C, 4), not input(_, C2, 4), C2 > C.

% Output size is the bounding box size
n_rows(N) :- N = max_4_row(R) - min_4_row(R) + 1.
n_cols(C) :- C = max_4_col(C) - min_4_col(C) + 1.

% Output cells
output_cell(R, C) :- R < n_rows(N), C < n_cols(C).

% Determine the color of each output cell
% ...
```

<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

```asp
% Find the canvas defined by 4s
min_4_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, Col, 4)}.
max_4_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, Col, 4)}.
min_4_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(Row, Col, 4)}.
max_4_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(Row, Col, 4)}.

% Canvas dimensions
canvas_height(H) :- H = max_4_row(Rmax) - min_4_row(Rmin) + 1.
canvas_width(W) :- W = max_4_col(Cmax) - min_4_col(Cmin) + 1.

% Output is the canvas
output_cell(R, C) :- R < canvas_height(H), C < canvas_width(W).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% 4s at the corners are preserved
:- min_4_row(Rmin), min_4_col(Cmin), output(0, 0, Color), Color != 4.
:- min_4_row(Rmin), max_4_col(Cmax), output(0, W-1, Color), Color != 4.
:- max_4_row(Rmax), min_4_col(Cmin), output(H-1, 0, Color), Color != 4.
:- max_4_row(Rmax), max_4_col(Cmax), output(H-1, W-1, Color), Color != 4.

% Non-4 cells within the canvas are taken from the input
:- input(R, C, V), min_4_row(Rmin), max_4_row(Rmax), min_4_col(Cmin), max_4_col(Cmax), Rmin <= R <= Rmax, Cmin <= C <= Cmax, V != 4, V != 0,
   DR = R - Rmin, DC = C - Cmin,
   output(DR, DC, Color), Color != V.

% Non-zero cells within the canvas are taken from the input
:- input(R, C, V), min_4_row(Rmin), max_4_row(Rmax), min_4_col(Cmin), max_4_col(Cmax), Rmin <= R <= Rmax, Cmin <= C <= Cmax, V != 0,
   DR = R - Rmin, DC = C - Cmin,
   output(DR, DC, Color), Color != V.

% Zero cells within the canvas are 0 in the output
:- input(R, C, 0), min_4_row(Rmin), max_4_row(Rmax), min_4_col(Cmin), max_4_col(Cmax), Rmin <= R <= Rmax, Cmin <= C <= Cmax,
   DR = R - Rmin, DC = C - Cmin,
   output(DR, DC, Color), Color != 0.
```

```asp
% Find the canvas defined by 4s
min_4_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, Col, 4)}.

max_4_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(Row, Col, 4)}.

canvas_height(H) :- H = max_4_row(Rmax) - min_4_row(Rmin) + 1.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
```

```asp
% Find the canvas defined by 4s
min_4_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, Col, 4)}.
max_4_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, Col, 4)}.
min_4_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(Row, Col, 4)}.
max_4_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(Row, Col, 4)}.

% Canvas dimensions
canvas_height(H) :- H = max_4_row(Rmax) - min_4_row(Rmin) + 1.
canvas_width(W) :- W = max_4_col(Cmax) - min_4_col(Cmin) + 1.

% Output is the canvas
output_cell(R, C) :- R < canvas_height(H), C < canvas_width(W).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% 4s at the corners are preserved
:- min_4_row(Rmin), min_4_col(Cmin), output(0, 0, Color), Color != 4.
:- min_4_row(Rmin), max_4_col(Cmax), output(0, W-1, Color), Color != 4.
:- max_4_row(Rmax), min_4_col(Cmin), output(H-1, 0, Color), Color != 4.
:- max_4_row(Rmax), max_4_col(Cmax), output(H-1, W-1, Color), Color != 4.

% Outside cells (outside the canvas, excluding 4s)
```

<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

Based on my analysis, here's my ASP program:

```asp
% Find the four 4s
four(R, C) :- input(R, C, 4).

% Determine the rectangle defined by the 4s
min_row(MR) :- MR = #min{R : four(R, _)}.
max_row(MR) :- MR = #max{R : four(R, _)}.
min_col(MC) :- MC = #min{C : four(_, C)}.
max_col(MC) :- MC = #max{C : four(_, C)}.

% Output has the same size as the rectangle
n_rows(NR) :- NR = MR - min_row + 1.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = MC - min_col + 1.

% Output cell exists
output_cell(R, C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C).

output_row(R) :-
    min_row(MR), NR = NR,
    R = MR + R.
output_col(C) :-
    min_col(MC), NC = NC,
    C = MC + C.
```

Based on my analysis, here's my ASP program:

```asp
% Find the four 4s
four(R, C) :- input(R, C, 4).

% Determine the rectangle defined by the 4s
min_row(MR) :- MR = #min{R : four(R, _)}.
max_row(MR) :- MR = #max{R : four(R, _)}.
min_col(MC) :- MC = #min{C : four(_, C)}.
max_col(MC) :- MC = #max{C : four(_, C)}.

% Output has the same size as the rectangle
output(R, C, Color) :-
    R >= MR, R =< MR,
    C >= MC, C =< MC,
    four(R, C), Color = 4.

% For each non-4, non-zero color, copy the inside objects to the output
output(R, C, Color) :-
    input(R, C, Color), Color != 4,
    R >= MR, R =< MR,
    C >= MC, C =< MC.

% For each non-4, non-zero color with outside objects, fill the gaps with that color
% ...
```

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

```asp
% Bounding box defined by 4s
box_min_r(R) :- input(R, _, 4).
box_max_r(R) :- input(R, _, 4).
box_min_c(C) :- input(_, C, 4).
box_max_c(C) :- input(_, C, 4).

% Output dimensions
box_height(N) :- N = #count{R : box_min_r(R), box_max_r(R), R >= box_min_r, R <= box_max_r}.
box_width(M) :- M = #count{C : box_min_c(C), box_max_c(C), C >= box_min_c, C <= box_max_c}.

% Output cells
output_cell(R, C) :-
    box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2), box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
    R = BR + R', C = BC + C',
    R' < box_height, C' < box_width.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
```

```asp
% Inside objects: copy to output
:- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0, Color != 4,
   box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2), box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
   R >= BR, R <= BR2, C >= BC, C <= BC2,
   R' = R - BR, C' = C - BC,
   output(R', C', ActualColor), ActualColor != Color.

% Outside objects: reflect into box
```

```asp
% Bounding box defined by 4s
box_min_r(R) :- input(R, _, 4).
box_max_r(R) :- input(R, _, 4).
box_min_c(C) :- input(_, C, 4).
box_max_c(C) :- input(_, C, 4).

% Output dimensions
box_height(N) :- N = #count{R : box_min_r(R), box_max_r(R), R >= box_min_r, R <= box_max_r}.
box_width(M) :- M = #count{C : box_min_c(C), box_max_c(C), C >= box_min_c, C <= box_max_c}.

% Output cells
output_cell(R, C) :-
    box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2), box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
    R = BR + R', C = BC + C',
    R' < box_height, C' < box_width.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Inside objects: copy to output
:- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0, Color != 4,
   box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2), box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
   R >= BR, R <= BR2, C >= BC, C <= BC2,
   R' = R - BR, C' = C - BC,
   output(R', C', ActualColor), ActualColor != Color.

% Outside objects: reflect into box
% For an outside cell (R, C), reflect it across the nearest box edge
reflect_row(R, R2) :-
    box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2),
    R > BR2, R2 = 2*BR2 - R.
reflect_row(R, R2) :-
    box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2),
    R < BR, R2 = 2*BR - R.
reflect_row(R, R) :-
    box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2),
    R >= BR, R <= BR2.

reflect_col(C, C2) :-
    box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
    C > BC2, C2 = 2*BC2 - C.
reflect_col(C, C2) :-
    box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
    C < BC, C2 = 2*BC - C.
reflect_col(C, C) :-
    box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
    C >= BC, C <= BC2.

% Copy outside objects to output (reflected)
:- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0, Color != 4,
   box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2), box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
   (R < BR ; R > BR2 ; C < BC ; C > BC2),
   reflect_row(R, R2), reflect_col(C, C2),
   R' = R2 - BR, C' = C2 - BC,
   output(R', C', ActualColor), ActualColor != Color.

% Default: output cell is 0
:- output_cell(R, C), not output(R, C, _).
```

Actually, I think the issue is that I'm overcomplicating the reflection. Let me step back and think about what the reflection should be.

For an outside cell (R, C), the reflection is:
- If R > BR2 (outside below): R2 = BR2 - (R - BR2) = 2*BR2 - R.
- If R < BR (outside above): R2 = BR + (BR - R) = 2*BR - R.
- If C > BC2 (outside right): C2 = BC2 - (C - BC2) = 2*BC2 - C.
- If C < BC (outside left): C2 = BC + (BC - C) = 2*BC - C.

For a cell that's outside both vertically and horizontally (e.g., bottom-right), we reflect both R and C.

Let me code this up more carefully.

```asp
% Bounding box defined by 4s
box_min_r(R) :- input(R, _, 4).
box_max_r(R) :- input(R, _, 4).
box_min_c(C) :- input(_, C, 4).
box_max_c(C) :- input(_, C, 4).

% Output dimensions
box_height(N) :- N = #count{R : box_min_r(R), box_max_r(R), R >= box_min_r, R <= box_max_r}.
box_width(M) :- M = #count{C : box_min_c(C), box_max_c(C), C >= box_min_c, C <= box_max_c}.

% Output cells
output_cell(R, C) :-
    box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2), box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
    R = BR + R', C = BC + C',
    R' < box_height, C' < box_width.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Inside objects: copy to output
:- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0, Color != 4,
   box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2), box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
   R >= BR, R <= BR2, C >= BC, C <= BC2,
   R' = R - BR, C' = C - BC,
   output(R', C', ActualColor), ActualColor != Color.

% Outside objects: reflect into box
% For an outside cell (R, C), compute reflected position
% First, determine if outside and by how much
outside_r_above(R) :- box_min_r(BR), R < BR.
outside_r_below(R) :- box_max_r(BR2), R > BR2.
outside_c_left(C) :- box_min_c(BC), C < BC.
outside_c_right(C) :- box_max_c(BC2), C > BC2.

% Compute reflected row
reflected_row(R, R2) :- outside_r_above(R), box_min_r(BR), R2 = 2*BR - R.
reflected_row(R, R2) :- outside_r_below(R), box_max_r(BR2), R2 = 2*BR2 - R.
reflected_row(R, R) :- not outside_r_above(R), not outside_r_below(R).

% Compute reflected column
reflected_col(C, C2) :- outside_c_left(C), box_min_c(BC), C2 = 2*BC - C.
reflected_col(C, C2) :- outside_c_right(C), box_max_c(BC2), C2 = 2*BC2 - C.
reflected_col(C, C) :- not outside_c_left(C), not outside_c_right(C).

% Copy reflected outside objects to output
:- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0, Color != 4,
   box_min_r(BR), box_max_r(BR2), box_min_c(BC), box_max_c(BC2),
   reflected_row(R, R2), reflected_col(C, C2),
   R' = R2 - BR, C' = C2 - BC,
   output(R', C', ActualColor), ActualColor != Color.

% Default: output cell is 0
:- output_cell(R, C), not output(R, C, _).
```

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>
